nouns.summary: The text states that Ἰούδας is the person who begot (ἤγένησεν) τὸν Φαρὲς and τὸν Ζαρὰ, with their origin given as being from Θάμαρ.
Φαρὲς is described as being begotten by Ἰούδας, and the text further states that Φαρὲς begot Ἑσρώμ.
Ζαρὰ is described as being begotten by Ἰούδας, with the text specifying that this was from Θάμαρ.
Θάμαρ is named as the person “from” whom Ἰούδας begot Φαρὲς and Ζαρὰ.
Ἑσρώμ is stated to be begotten by Φαρὲς, and it is further said that Ἑσρώμ begot Ἀράμ.
Ἀράμ is the child who is begotten by Ἑσρώμ, as given in the genealogical sequence in the text.
